Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal: The objective is to transform raw ERP tables into a structured event log suitable for process mining. Analyze the Input Data: Orders Defines the start of the process. Order Lines Defines picking fulfillment details. Delivery Orders can be delivered in multiple shipments. Invoice Orders may generate one or more invoices. Payments Invoices may be paid in one or multiple installments.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Challenge: One order has multiple lines with potentially different picking dates. Need to decide whether to create one Picked event per order or per line. Process mining usually looks at the case lifecycle. Picking is often a batch activity per order, but the data shows individual line picking times.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Option A: One event Order Picked per order. Timestamp = Max(Picking Date). Option B: Multiple events Item Picked per order. Prompt guidance: Items Picked event(s) produce one event per activity if its meaningful. Aggregate the picking activity to the order level for the sake of a clean process flow.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Decision: To ensure chronological correctness and distinct activities, create one event per logical step per case. For picking, aggregate to single Order Picked event per order using the latest picking date as the order isnt ready until the last item is picked. This note assumption.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step-by-Step Construction Plan: Event 1: Order Created Source: Orders Table. Activity: Order Created. Resource: Sales Person. Timestamp: order_date. Event 2: Order Picked Source: Order Lines. Timestamp: Max picking date per order. Event 3: Order Dispatched Source: Delivery. Event 4: Order Arrived. Event 5: Invoice Issued. Event 6: Payment Received join via invoice_id.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For process mining, having multiple events with the same activity name for the same case ID is valid but for a general Order-to-Cash log, a single Picking event per case is standard practice. Aggregate to the Order level for the Picking phase unless analysis requires item-level.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction on Order Picked: The prompt says Items Picked event(s). If produce one per line item the log becomes very long. However if aggregate lose granularity. Decision: aggregate to a single Order Picked event per Order to maintain the standard Order-to-Cash process flow structure.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Data Verification: Order 1004 has no Delivery Invoice Payment. Include it stops at Picked. Order 1002 has 2 Deliveries 2 Invoices. Chronology Check: timestamps align for all orders. Final Review: Case ID order_id yes. Timestamps chronological yes. Every event included yes.
